name: The gorges of la Carança - loop starting from Thuès-Entre-Valls - Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
name_original: Les gorges de la Carança – boucle au départ de Thuès-Entre-Valls – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
region_intro: Hiking around Thuès-Entre-Valls offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. The region is characterized by deep gorges, such as the Gorges de la Carança, with rushing torrents and dense forests. Unique natural features include thermal hot springs and warm waterfalls, providing distinct points of interest for hikers. The terrain varies from river valleys to more elevated paths, with prominent peaks like Mont Canigou shaping the distant views. Best hiking trails around Thuès-Entre-Valls The most popular hiking route is The gorges of la Carança - loop starting from Thuès-Entre-Valls - Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate loop explores the dramatic Gorges de la Carança. Another top favourite among local hikers is Carança Corniche – Gorges de la Carança loop from Thuès-Carança, a difficult 13.3 miles (21.5 km) path. This challenging route offers extensive views along the Carança Corniche. Local hikers also love the Hot springs – Warm Waterfall loop from Thuès-Entre-Valls, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through natural hot springs, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Thuès-Entre-Valls is defined by spectacular gorges, natural hot springs, and varied mountain terrain within the Pyrénées Catalanes. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to demanding multi-day treks. The routes in Thuès-Entre-Valls are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 7,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Thuès-Entre-Valls's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Thuès-Entre-Valls?
A: The terrain around Thuès-Entre-Valls is incredibly varied, ranging from deep river valleys and dramatic gorges like the &lt;strong&gt;Gorges de la Carança&lt;/strong&gt; to more elevated mountain paths. You'll encounter dense forests, rushing torrents, and even paths carved directly into rock faces. The distant views are often shaped by prominent peaks such as Mont Canigou.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Thuès-Entre-Valls offers several easy trails. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20068360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hot springs – Warm Waterfall loop from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.7 km long and leads through natural hot springs. Another accessible route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38652937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche – Hängebrücke loop from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.1 km path that includes suspended footbridges.

Q: Can I find trails with unique natural features like hot springs or waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its natural hot springs and warm waterfalls at Thuès-les-Bains. You can explore these unique features on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20068360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hot springs – Warm Waterfall loop from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt;. The Gorges de la Carança also feature rushing torrents and dramatic rock formations.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced hikers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1077252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche – Gorges de la Carança loop from Thuès-Carança&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.5 km route offering extensive views along the Carança Corniche. This trail demands good physical condition and sure-footedness.

Q: What are the Gorges de la Carança like for hiking?
A: The Gorges de la Carança offer an adventurous hiking experience. Trails wind through the gorges, featuring aerial passages, footbridges, and corniche paths carved into rock faces. You can find routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958786372/the-gorges-of-la-caranca-loop-starting-from-thues-entre-valls-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-catalanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The gorges of la Carança - loop starting from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.2 km loop, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14953233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path through overhanging rock – Gorges de la Carança loop from Thuès-Carança&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.5 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Thuès-Entre-Valls?
A: The hiking trails in Thuès-Entre-Valls are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the spectacular gorges, the unique experience of the natural hot springs, and the varied mountain terrain within the Pyrénées Catalanes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several impressive landmarks and viewpoints. You can find stunning mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4973719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastiments Peak (2,881 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Colom summit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2522979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marrana Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views. The Gorges de la Carança themselves provide breathtaking high-altitude vistas.

Q: Are there any places to stay overnight or refuges along longer trails?
A: For multi-day treks or longer excursions, there are huts and refuges available. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3109668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ras de la Carança Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/171997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldeter Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. These provide essential shelter for hikers exploring the more remote areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Thuès-Entre-Valls?
A: Thuès-Entre-Valls offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. The natural hot springs are particularly enjoyable even during colder months due to their warm temperatures. For higher altitude trails, late spring to early autumn generally provides the most favorable conditions, avoiding heavy snow or extreme heat.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Thuès-Entre-Valls?
A: There are 18 verified hiking routes available around Thuès-Entre-Valls. These trails cater to various fitness levels, with 8 easy, 7 moderate, and 3 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the popular trails in Thuès-Entre-Valls are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958786372/the-gorges-of-la-caranca-loop-starting-from-thues-entre-valls-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-catalanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The gorges of la Carança - loop starting from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20068360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hot springs – Warm Waterfall loop from Thuès-Entre-Valls&lt;/a&gt;.

The passages along the Corniche de Carança are quite dizzying and spectacular. Attention therefore for those who have vertigo or with children.
